packet:c4468836-8b47-4cde-9985-2e6ac4f96.title: Flight Deck is a docs-heavy monorepo with two stable runtime domains: `kb-server` (FastAPI + Git/PR workflow + autosave worker) and `vault-sync` (local mirror that reads `view=current` and pushes human edits as direct-to-main writes). `main` is aligned with `origin/main` at `7c47d50` (`docs: add local pre-commit checks and stale-doc autofix`); the only local worktree difference is an untracked `.control-tower/` bootstrap tree. Recent mainline evolution on March 7, 2026 concentrated on documentation/context-system rollout closure, local docs automation, and non-interactive git auth hardening. Remote branches indicate likely next workstreams beyond `main`: role-based/hashed API auth, actor-aware git identities, a PR evaluation harness, deterministic retrieval APIs, and an MCP adapter. Main risks for Tower: code/doc drift around readiness semantics, stale control-tower memory that has not curated recent repo changes, and a broken local test runner environment that prevented runtime validation.

dec_inferred_c4468836-8b47-4cde-9985-2e6.title: As of 2026-03-22, local branch is `main`, aligned with `origin/main` at `7c47d50`; the only local worktree delta is untracked `.control-tower/`.
dec_inferred_c4468836-8b47-4cde-9985-2e6.summary: Inferred from ResultPacket memory_worthy entry: As of 2026-03-22, local branch is `main`, aligned with `origin/main` at `7c47d50`; the only local worktree delta is untracked `.control-tower/`.
rationale: Captured from a ResultPacket memory_worthy entry pending curation.

question:which-of-the-remote-branches-ah.title: Which of the remote branches ahead of `main` should Tower treat as active project direction versus parked exploration?

packet:2d4cb429-929d-4fb7-9449-ba186b7df.title: Remote work ahead of `main` breaks into three active lines and one parked exploratory stack. Active lines are: auth hardening (`feat/actor-aware-git-identities` -> `feat/hashed-api-key-auth`), Tower/automation infrastructure (`feat/pr-eval-harness`), and retrieval (`yashom7/revup/main/retrieval-layer`). The MCP adapter branch is still exploratory because it depends on retrieval, introduces a new top-level `mcp-server/` domain, and is only open as a stacked PR against the retrieval branch. `docs/PLANS.md` drift is real and the smallest corrective action is to restore `docs/exec-plans/active/` as an empty tracked directory or placeholder README rather than rewriting multiple docs references. Recommended immediate Tower action: route Git-master to land only the missing `docs/exec-plans/active/` restoration from the existing auth branch commit, then continue curation from a clean doc state.

dec_inferred_2d4cb429-929d-4fb7-9449-ba1.title: Remote branches ahead of `main` now classify into active auth stack (`feat/actor-aware-git-identities` -> `feat/hashed-api-key-auth`), active infra (`feat/pr-eval-harness`), active candidate retrieval line (`yashom7/revup/main/retrieval-layer`), and parked dependent exploration (`yashom7/revup/main/mcp-adapter`).
dec_inferred_2d4cb429-929d-4fb7-9449-ba1.summary: Inferred from ResultPacket memory_worthy entry: Remote branches ahead of `main` now classify into active auth stack (`feat/actor-aware-git-identities` -> `feat/hashed-api-key-auth`), active infra (`feat/pr-eval-harness`), active candidate retrieval line (`yashom7/revup/main/retrieval-layer`), and parked dependent exploration (`yashom7/revup/main/mcp-adapter`).
